# 11a Terminology — High-Frequency Codelists — core subdir (Stage v2.4)

High-frequency CDISC controlled terminology codelists sourced from ``knowledge_base/terminology/core/``. Each codelist preserves its 4-column Term table (Code / Submission Value / Synonyms / Definition) with Definitions truncated to 200 characters. "Related Domains" is a codelist-level metadata line listing SDTM domains whose spec.md references the codelist C-code.

<!-- source: knowledge_base/terminology/core/general_part4.md -->
## No Yes Response (C66742)

Extensible: No
Related Domains: AE, AG, BS, CE, CM, CP, CV, DM, EC, EG, EX, FA, FT, GF, HO, IE, IS, LB, MB, MH, MI, MK, ML, MS, NV, OE, PC, PE, PR, QS, RE, RP, RS, SR, SU, SV, TM, TR, TU, UR, VS

| Code | CDISC Submission Value | CDISC Synonym(s) | CDISC Definition |
|------|----------------------|-------------------|------------------|
| C49487 | N | No | The non-affirmative response to a question. (NCI) |
| C48660 | NA | NA; Not Applicable | Determination of a value is not relevant in the current context. (NCI) |
| C17998 | U | U; UNK; Unknown | Not known, not observed, not recorded, or refused. (NCI) |
| C49488 | Y | Yes | The affirmative response to a question. (NCI) |

| Code | CDISC Submission Value | CDISC Synonym(s) | CDISC Definition |
|------|----------------------|-------------------|------------------|
| C125938 | BASELINE |  | A period in a clinical study after eligibility has been met and before the start of treatment, at which baseline measurements are collected. |
| C102255 | BLINDED TREATMENT |  | A period in a clinical study during which subjects receive blinded therapeutic treatment. |
| C123452 | CONTINUATION TREATMENT | Continuation Phase | A period in a clinical study during which subjects receive continuation treatment. |
| C99158 | FOLLOW-UP |  | A period in a clinical study during which information about the health status of an individual is obtained after study interventions have concluded. |
| C123453 | INDUCTION TREATMENT | Induction Phase; Intensive Phase | A period in a clinical study during which subjects receive induction treatment. |
| C16032 | LONG-TERM FOLLOW-UP |  | A period in a clinical study during which information about the health status of an individual is obtained long after study interventions have concluded. |
| C165873 | OBSERVATION |  | A period in a clinical study during which subjects are observed, without any planned intervention. |
| C102256 | OPEN LABEL TREATMENT |  | A period in a clinical study during which subjects receive open label therapeutic treatment. |
| C98779 | RUN-IN |  | A period in a clinical study that occurs before the commencement of the primary clinical study investigation, during which subjects may be assessed for suitability in the clinical study, withdrawn... |
| C48262 | SCREENING |  | A period in a clinical study during which subjects are evaluated for participation in the study. |
| C101526 | TREATMENT |  | A period in a study during which subjects are receiving investigational therapy or treatment. |
| C42872 | WASHOUT |  | A period of time during a study when a subject is taken off of the investigational therapy or treatment in order to reduce the amount of investigational product within the body. |

| C78725 | AUTOLYZED | Autolyzed Specimen | A specimen that has undergone autolysis, or self-digestion by the specimen's own digestive enzymes. (NCI) |

| C184709 | CAUTERIZED |  | A specimen that has been cauterized. |

| C68768 | CONTAMINATED | Contaminated Specimen | The presence of any substance or organism that makes a preparation impure. (NCI) |
| C84516 | DRIED | Dried Specimen | A specimen that has become dessicated or dehydrated. |
| C84517 | FRESH | Fresh Specimen | A specimen that is analyzed in the state that it was collected. |
| C70717 | FROZEN | Frozen Specimen | A specimen that has been subjected to and immobilized by severe cold. (NCI) |
| C135503 | GELLED | Gelled Specimen | A specimen that has a gelatinous consistency. (NCI) |
| C70720 | HEMOLYZED | Hemolysis in Specimen | A specimen that has undergone the destruction of red blood cells followed by the release of the hemoglobin. (NCI) |
| C98744 | ICTERIC | Icteric Specimen | A specimen that exhibits a yellowish pigmentation due to jaundice. (NCI) |
| C158278 | LACTESCENT | Lactescent Specimen | A specimen that has become or appears milky. |
| C70715 | LIPEMIC | Lipemic Specimen | A specimen that consists of or contains excessive amounts of fat and fatty substances. (NCI) |
| C158279 | NON-HEMOLYZED | Non-Hemolyzed Specimen | A specimen that has not undergone the destruction of red blood cells followed by the release of the hemoglobin. |
| C19597 | PARAFFIN-EMBEDDED | Paraffin Block; Paraffin-Embedded Specimen | A specimen that has been fixed and preserved in paraffin. |
| C70718 | REFRIGERATED | Refrigerated Specimen | A specimen that has been kept or preserved at a low temperature in a refrigerator. (NCI) |
| C70719 | ROOM TEMPERATURE | Ambient Temperature; Specimen at Room Temperature | A specimen that has been subjected to and adjusted to the average ambient temperature of a room, usually considered to be around 20 degrees C (68 degrees F). (NCI) |
| C135504 | SOLIDIFIED | Solidified Specimen | A specimen that has a regular, firm consistency and retains a definite size and shape. |
| C158280 | THAWED | Thawed Specimen | A specimen that has changed from a frozen to a liquid or semi-liquid state. |
| C135505 | UNEXPECTED ODOR | Specimen with Unexpected Odor | A specimen that has an unanticipated odor. |

| C189448 | NORMALIZED NUMBER OF SEQUENCE VARIANTS |  | A quantitative assessment of the number of sequence variants within a region of interest, which has been normalized. |
| C45374 | NUCLEOTIDE SEQUENCE |  | The sequence of nucleotides within a genetic region of interest. |
| C181348 | NUMBER OF ALTERED EXONS |  | The number of affected exons as compared to the reference sequence. |
